content.text:
{In my Father's house are many rooms.}{John 14:2}
 {A mathematician, like a painter or poet, is a maker of patterns.}{— G. H. Hardy}

A **subspace** is a vector space that lives inside a larger 
vector space.

content.title: Subspace
content.content:
A subset $H$ of a vector space $V$ is a **subspace** 
of $V$ if $H$ is itself a vector space under the same operations as $V$.

content.text:
Fortunately, we do not need to verify all eight axioms. Most are 
inherited automatically from the parent space.

content.title: Subspace Test
content.content:
A subset $H$ of a vector space $V$ is a subspace if and only if:

1. $\vect{0} \in H$ (contains the zero vector)

2. If $\vect{u}, \vect{v} \in H$, then $\vect{u} + \vect{v} \in H$ 
 (closed under addition)

3. If $\vect{v} \in H$ and $c \in \R$, then $c\vect{v} \in H$ 
 (closed under scalar multiplication)
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Subspace examples
content.problem:
1. In $\R^3$: any plane through the origin is a subspace. Any 
 line through the origin is a subspace.

2. In $\mathcal{P}_3$: the set of polynomials with $p(0) = 0$ 
 is a subspace.

3. In $M_{2 \times 2}$: the set of symmetric matrices is a subspace.

4. For any matrix $A$: the solution set of $A\vect{x} = \vect{0}$ 
 is a subspace of $\R^n$ (the **null space**).

content.title: Non-subspaces
content.problem:
1. In $\R^2$: the line $y = x + 1$ is not a subspace (does not 
 contain the origin).

2. In $\R^2$: the first quadrant $\{(x,y) : x \geq 0, y \geq 0\}$ 
 is not a subspace (not closed under scalar multiplication by $-1$).

3. The set of invertible $2 \times 2$ matrices is not a subspace 
 of $M_{2 \times 2}$ (does not contain the zero matrix).

content.title: Column Space and Null Space
content.content:
For an $m \times n$ matrix $A$:

- The **column space** $\Col(A)$ is 
 the span of the columns of $A$. It is a subspace of $\R^m$.

- The **null space** $\Null(A)$ is the 
 set of all solutions to $A\vect{x} = \vect{0}$. It is a subspace 
 of $\R^n$.

content.title: Column Space Characterization
content.content:
$\vect{b}$ is in $\Col(A)$ if and only if $A\vect{x} = \vect{b}$ has 
a solution.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Finding the null space
content.solution:
Row reduce: $A \to \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$.

From $x_1 + 3x_2 + 4x_3 = 0$ with $x_2 = s$, $x_3 = t$ free:
\[
\Null(A) = \left\{ s\begin{bmatrix} -3 \\ 1 \\ 0 \end{bmatrix} + 
t\begin{bmatrix} -4 \\ 0 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix} : s, t \in \R \right\}
\]
This is a plane in $\R^3$.

marginNotes.text:
And God saw that the light was good. 
And God separated the light from the darkness.
